messages.content:
Does this correct for REINFORCE: L(\theta)&=-\mathbb{E}_{a \sim \pi_\theta}\left[R(a)\right], \\
    &=-\sum_{a} \pi_\theta(a) R(a). \nabla_\theta L(\theta)&=-\mathbb{E}\left[R(a) \nabla_\theta \log \pi_{\theta} \right].

Almost—your final gradient is missing the action argument inside the log, and it’s clearer to keep the expectation explicitly over \(a\sim \pi_\theta\).

Starting from
\[
L(\theta)=-\mathbb{E}_{a\sim \pi_\theta}[R(a)]
= -\sum_a \pi_\theta(a)\,R(a),
\]
then
\[
\nabla_\theta L(\theta)
= -\sum_a R(a)\,\nabla_\theta \pi_\theta(a)
= -\sum_a R(a)\,\pi_\theta(a)\,\nabla_\theta \log \pi_\theta(a)
= -\mathbb{E}_{a\sim \pi_\theta}\big[\,R(a)\,\nabla_\theta \log \pi_\theta(a)\,\big].
\]

So the correct REINFORCE form is:
\[
\nabla_\theta L(\theta)= -\mathbb{E}_{a\sim \pi_\theta}\left[R(a)\nabla_\theta \log \pi_\theta(a)\right].
\]

(And in practice you often use a baseline: replace \(R(a)\) by \(R(a)-b\) to reduce variance.)
